name: Electel Cooperative
description_short: ElecTel Cooperative is a federally insured credit union offering member-owned financial services including savings accounts, loans, and Visa cards with competitive rates.
description_long:
ElecTel Cooperative Federal Credit Union is a member-owned, not-for-profit financial institution federally insured by the National Credit Union Administration (NCUA). Based in Raleigh, North Carolina, the credit union has been serving its membership community with traditional and digital banking services. The organization operates with a focus on member benefits and competitive rates rather than shareholder profits.

ElecTel offers a comprehensive range of consumer financial products including share savings accounts, checking accounts, money market accounts, share certificates and IRAs, and various loan products. Their loan portfolio includes vehicle loans, energy efficient loans, unsecured personal loans, home equity lines of credit, and collateral-based loans. Members can also access a Visa credit card, online and mobile banking platforms, direct deposit services, wire transfers, and audio response services through their Starline system. The credit union provides rate calculators and current rate information to help members evaluate loan and savings options.

The credit union distinguishes itself through digital-first member services, offering a free mobile app for iOS and Android devices that provides 24/7 account access, balance checking, fund transfers, and bill payment capabilities. ElecTel emphasizes member privacy and security, with explicit statements that they will never request personal information via email, voice, chat, or text. The organization maintains active member engagement through annual membership meetings and scholarship programs, and offers multiple contact channels including phone, text (919-443-6144), and physical branch locations.

As a federally insured credit union, ElecTel is subject to NCUA regulation and operates under the cooperative credit union model. The website provides standard disclosures, fee schedules, and forms for member access. However, the website content does not include detailed information about membership eligibility requirements, specific loan terms, current rates, or minimum deposit requirements, which would be useful for prospective members evaluating the institution.
services: Direct deposit, wire transfers, and check services
pros: Federally insured by NCUA with equal housing lender status, providing member deposit protection
Free mobile app with 24/7 account access, fund transfers, and bill payment for iOS and Android
Multiple contact channels including phone (919-872-2395 or 1-800-849-5600), text messaging, and local branch
Member-owned cooperative structure means profits benefit members rather than external shareholders
Diverse loan product offerings including vehicle loans, home equity lines of credit, and energy efficient loans
Active member engagement programs including annual membership meetings and scholarship opportunities
Commitment to member privacy with explicit security statements regarding credential protection
cons: Limited geographic accessibility with primary location in Raleigh, NC; membership eligibility and service areas not clearly defined on website
Website lacks specific information about current loan rates, APRs, and terms needed for rate comparison with competitors
No details provided about membership eligibility requirements or minimum deposit thresholds
Limited transparency about loan approval criteria, processing times, or specific features of offered products
Website does not clearly specify if services are available nationwide online or restricted to local/state membership
best_for: Members in the North Carolina area seeking federally insured savings and loan products through a not-for-profit institution
Consumers looking for member-owned financial services with emphasis on competitive rates and member benefits
People preferring 24/7 digital banking access through mobile app alongside traditional in-branch services
diagnosis: ElecTel is best for consumers seeking federally insured member-owned financial services in or near the Raleigh, NC area who value competitive rates and digital-first banking options. The main caveat is that the website lacks specific rate and eligibility information needed to evaluate whether ElecTel's products are competitive or accessible for individual prospective members.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Electel Cooperative — Raleigh, NC Credit Union | CreditDoc
meta_description: Electel Cooperative — NCUA-insured credit union in Raleigh, NC. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
